sourceScope.primarySourceFile: AG 05 - Acceptable Approximations for Continuous Functions.pdf
sourceScope.sourceTitle: AG 05 - Acceptable Approximations for Continuous Functions

observedSectionWindows.note: Two-page approximation guideline for continuous functions and reserve/value calculations.

boundaries: Keep planning focused on this single AG 05 PDF.
Do not widen into other Actuarial Guidelines files in the same folder.
Keep the continuous-function approximation language review-only until a human confirms the indexing decision.
Keep any PDF text-extraction noise visible as a caveat, not a promotion signal.
Keep the batch source-bound and review-only.
exclusions: No eLearning scripts, slides, quizzes, or app exports.
No raw source files in Git.
No other guideline files in the same wave.

topicMap.label: Acceptable approximations for continuous functions
topicMap.boundaryNote: Keep this slice within pages 1-2 and do not absorb other guideline files.

proposedBatches.title: Two-page AG 05 interpretation

pageTarget.notes: Capture the whole two-page guideline as a single review-only slice and stop at the page boundary.

proposedBatches.rationale: Capture the full two-page guideline as a single review-only slice and stop at the page boundary.

reviewStandards.regulatoryRequirement: Treat explicit must, shall, or required language as the requirement; preserve the citation and source context.
reviewStandards.definitionOrTerminology: Treat continuous functions, approximation language, and the actuarial-function references as terminology content unless they impose a duty.
reviewStandards.formulaContext: Treat the reserve/value approximation formulas as context that needs source-bound preservation rather than promotion.
reviewStandards.crossReferenceMapping: Treat references to reserves, values, mortality, or interest-rate language as mapping notes unless the operational text is included.
reviewStandards.backgroundContent: Treat the title line and framing sentence as background content unless they impose a duty.
reviewStandards.boundaryControlWindow: Treat the two-page slice as a review-only window that should not be merged forward.
reviewStandards.requiresHumanInterpretation: Treat the indexing choice and the OCR / encoded text layer as requiring human actuarial interpretation unless the text is explicit.
reviewStandards.sourceQualityCaveat: Treat text-layer noise or encoding issues as review-only caveats and do not widen the batch because of them.

appReady: export is sanitized, stable, and versioned
ragReady: review status no longer requires human disposition

currentChecksNeeded: Plan file exists and parses as JSON.
The AG 05 plan markdown exists and carries the expected headings.
Every proposed batch is review-only by default.
The plan keeps the scope to two pages only.
Each planned batch has a matching batch definition.
The AG 05 batch definition file stays synchronized with the plan.
futureChecksSuggested: Two-page guideline checks for future short guideline files.
Cross-reference flag coverage for the approximation formulas.
No learner-facing, app-ready, or RAG-ready promotion checks.
Text-layer quality caveat checks once extraction starts.
scriptImplications: scripts/ag05-batch-definitions.mjs should stay synchronized with the planned batch-080 slice when extraction is authorized.
scripts/batch-definitions.mjs should import the AG 05 definition file so the shared runner can find the planned batch ID.
scripts/validate-scaffold.mjs should verify that the AG 05 plan file exists, parses cleanly, and preserves the review-only posture.
The shared runner should continue to honor the same stop conditions used for the VM-20 batches.

notes: Controlled AG 05 planning artifact. Planned batch 080 remains review-only by default and is not an extraction run.
